evals.prompt: I'm a PhD candidate in computational biology applying for postdoc positions at R1 universities. I have 4 publications (2 first-author in Nature Methods and Cell Systems), 3 conference presentations, received an NSF fellowship, and have TAed 3 courses. Build me an academic CV.
evals.expected_output: A structured academic CV with all standard sections formatted for research-focused postdoc applications at R1 institutions
expectations: Output includes standard CV sections: Education, Research Experience, Publications, Presentations, Grants/Fellowships, Teaching
Publications section distinguishes first-author from co-author works
Nature Methods and Cell Systems are cited with full bibliographic format
NSF fellowship is prominently featured in grants/awards section
Output uses academic CV conventions (not resume conventions — no objective statement, full dates, complete publication titles)

evals.prompt: I'm a tenured associate professor in sociology applying for a full professor position. I have 2 books, 18 journal articles, 5 grants totaling $2.1M, supervised 12 PhD students (7 graduated), and have significant service including department chair for 2 years. Create a comprehensive academic CV.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive multi-page academic CV appropriate for a senior faculty tenure/promotion dossier
expectations: Output includes books section distinct from journal articles
Grant funding is listed with amounts, funding agencies, and dates
PhD student supervision is listed with graduation status
Service section includes department chair with dates
Output reflects senior faculty profile with appropriate weight on scholarly impact

evals.prompt: I'm transitioning from industry (5 years as a data scientist at Google) to academia. I have a PhD in statistics, 2 publications from my dissertation, 1 industry co-authored paper, and I'm applying for lecturer and assistant professor positions. How should I format my CV and what should I emphasize?
evals.expected_output: Academic CV guidance and template for an industry-to-academia transition, with advice on framing industry experience for academic audiences
expectations: Output advises leading with academic credentials (PhD, publications) over industry titles
Output includes a research statement or research interests section
Industry experience is framed in terms of research contribution, not business metrics
Output addresses the gap in traditional academic markers (limited teaching, service) and how to handle it
Output recommends emphasizing any industry publications and open-source contributions
